generated_from: the ai-readiness-audit Python engine (exact strings; do not paraphrase)

levels.name: Ad hoc
levels.summary: No policy, no owner, no named risks; experimentation without structure.

levels.name: Emerging
levels.summary: Pockets of activity; informal ownership; risks unnamed.

levels.name: Defined
levels.summary: Policy and an accountable owner exist; risks named; basic controls; repeatable.

levels.name: Governed
levels.summary: Controls tested; measurement in place; data governed; human-in-loop by severity.

levels.name: Optimized
levels.summary: Continuous assurance; severity-weighted evaluation; autonomous-ready governance.

dimensions.name: Governance & Decision Rights
dimensions.core_question: Is there an accountable executive, a written AI policy, and defined approval gates?

questions.prompt: Who is accountable for AI outcomes in your organization?

level_anchors.1: No one is accountable; use is unsanctioned.
level_anchors.2: Informal — a champion advocates, but ownership is unassigned.
level_anchors.3: A named executive owns AI, documented in role charters.
level_anchors.4: A named owner plus a governing body that meets on a cadence.
level_anchors.5: Board-level oversight with reporting and continuous accountability.

questions.prompt: Is there a written AI policy governing acceptable use and approval?

level_anchors.1: No written policy of any kind.
level_anchors.3: A documented AI policy exists and is communicated.
level_anchors.4: Policy is enforced with defined approval gates by risk tier.
level_anchors.5: Policy is versioned, audited, and continuously improved.

questions.prompt: How are AI initiatives approved before they ship?

level_anchors.1: No approval step; anyone can deploy.
level_anchors.2: Ad hoc sign-off, case by case.
level_anchors.3: A defined intake and approval path exists.
level_anchors.4: Approval gates scale with assessed risk and blast radius.
level_anchors.5: Gated approvals with post-deployment review feeding the next gate.

dimensions.name: Risk & Control Posture
dimensions.core_question: Are AI-specific risks — hallucination, data leakage, prompt injection, blast radius, drift — named and mapped to controls with human-in-loop gates by severity?

questions.prompt: Are AI-specific risks named (hallucination, leakage, injection, drift)?

level_anchors.2: A few risks are discussed informally.
level_anchors.3: AI-specific risks are named and documented.
level_anchors.4: Named risks are mapped to controls and owners.
level_anchors.5: Risk register is live, severity-weighted, and reviewed on a cadence.

questions.prompt: Is there a human-in-the-loop gate calibrated to severity?

level_anchors.1: No human review of AI output.
level_anchors.3: Human review is defined for sensitive use.
level_anchors.4: Review depth scales with output severity.
level_anchors.5: Severity-tiered review with escalation and audit trail.

questions.prompt: How is model/output drift detected and contained?

level_anchors.1: Drift is neither defined nor monitored.
level_anchors.2: Drift is understood as a concept only.
level_anchors.3: Drift is monitored manually or periodically.
level_anchors.4: Automated drift signals trigger defined responses.
level_anchors.5: Continuous drift detection with rollback and containment runbooks.

dimensions.name: Data Readiness
dimensions.iso_lineage: ISO/IEC 42001 Annex A (Data for AI) + ISO/IEC 5259 (data quality)
dimensions.core_question: Is data accessible, governed, quality-assured, lineage-traceable, and rights/licensing-cleared for AI use?

questions.prompt: Is the data intended for AI accessible and inventoried?

level_anchors.1: Data location and ownership are unknown.
level_anchors.2: Some sources are known but not catalogued.
level_anchors.3: A data inventory exists for AI-relevant sources.
level_anchors.4: Inventory includes lineage and access controls.
level_anchors.5: Governed catalog with automated lineage and freshness.

questions.prompt: Is data quality assured for the data AI will consume?

level_anchors.3: Defined quality checks run before use.
level_anchors.4: Quality gates block low-quality data automatically.
level_anchors.5: Continuous quality monitoring with remediation SLAs.

questions.prompt: Are usage rights and licensing cleared for AI use of this data?

level_anchors.3: Rights are reviewed before AI use.
level_anchors.4: Licensing is tracked per dataset with constraints enforced.
level_anchors.5: Rights and licensing are continuously governed and auditable.

dimensions.name: Security & Privacy
dimensions.core_question: Are access controls, PII handling, model/data isolation, and third-party processing posture defined and disclosed?

questions.prompt: How is access to AI systems and their data controlled?

level_anchors.1: No access controls specific to AI.
level_anchors.3: Role-based access is defined.
level_anchors.4: Least-privilege access with periodic review.
level_anchors.5: Continuously enforced least privilege with just-in-time access.

questions.prompt: How is PII handled in AI workflows?

level_anchors.3: PII handling rules are defined for AI use.
level_anchors.4: PII is minimized, masked, or isolated by policy.
level_anchors.5: Automated PII controls with monitoring and disclosure.

questions.prompt: Is third-party model/data processing understood and disclosed?

level_anchors.3: Processing posture is documented.
level_anchors.4: Contracts and disclosures cover third-party processing.
level_anchors.5: Processing is governed, disclosed, and continuously verified.

dimensions.name: Talent & Operating Model
dimensions.core_question: Are skills present or sourced, roles defined (build / operate / govern), and run-state ownership assigned?

questions.prompt: Are the skills to build and run AI present or sourced?

level_anchors.3: Required skills are identified and being sourced.
level_anchors.4: Build/operate/govern roles are staffed.
level_anchors.5: A sustained capability with succession and development.

questions.prompt: Are build, operate, and govern responsibilities defined?

level_anchors.3: Roles are defined on paper.
level_anchors.4: Roles are staffed with clear handoffs.
level_anchors.5: Operating model is measured and continuously improved.

questions.prompt: Who owns an AI system once it is in production?

level_anchors.3: A run-state owner is named.
level_anchors.4: Owner has defined runbooks and on-call.
level_anchors.5: Full lifecycle ownership with SLOs and review.

dimensions.name: Third-Party & Concentration Risk
dimensions.core_question: Is model/vendor concentration understood, contractually controlled, and portable, with a bounded dependency blast radius?

questions.prompt: Is your model/vendor concentration understood?

level_anchors.3: Vendor and model dependencies are documented.
level_anchors.4: Concentration risk is assessed and bounded.
level_anchors.5: Concentration is continuously monitored with limits.

questions.prompt: Are third-party AI dependencies contractually controlled?

level_anchors.3: AI-specific terms exist in key contracts.
level_anchors.4: Terms cover data use, exit, and liability.
level_anchors.5: Contracts are governed with active risk management.

questions.prompt: How portable are you off a given model or vendor?

level_anchors.1: Fully locked in; no exit path.
level_anchors.3: A migration path is documented.
level_anchors.4: Portability is tested periodically.
level_anchors.5: Abstraction and drills keep switching cost bounded.

dimensions.name: Measurement & Assurance
dimensions.core_question: Can you evaluate outputs? Is there an eval harness? Do you measure failure severity, test real scenarios, and evaluate the evaluation itself?

questions.prompt: Can you evaluate the quality of AI outputs?

level_anchors.3: Defined evaluation criteria exist.
level_anchors.4: An eval harness runs against real scenarios.
level_anchors.5: Evaluation is continuous and severity-weighted.

questions.prompt: Do you measure the severity of failures, not just their rate?

level_anchors.3: Severity is assessed after incidents.
level_anchors.4: Severity is built into the evaluation.
level_anchors.5: Severity-weighted metrics drive control changes.

questions.prompt: Do you evaluate the evaluation itself for blind spots?

level_anchors.3: Periodic review of eval coverage.
level_anchors.4: Eval coverage gaps are tracked and closed.
level_anchors.5: Meta-evaluation is continuous and audited.

scoring.dimension_score_rule: Arithmetic mean of the answered question levels within the dimension (each weighted by question_weight; uniform).
scoring.overall_score_rule: Weighted mean of the seven dimension scores (each weighted by its dimension weight; uniform).
scoring.band_rule: Assign the highest level whose min_score <= score. Scores range 1.0..5.0.

gap_rule.per_dimension_sort: severity desc, then question_id asc; take the top N per dimension
gap_rule.overall_sort: priority_rank asc (priority dimensions = 0, others = 1), then severity desc, then dimension_order asc, then question_id asc

copy.headline: The AI-Readiness Standard
copy.subtitle: Seven dimensions. Five levels. One honest picture of where you stand.
copy.tagline: A deterministic AI-readiness diagnostic aligned to ISO/IEC 42001 domains and NIST AI RMF functions
copy.descriptor: An audit-grade readiness standard aligned to ISO/IEC 42001 domains and NIST AI RMF functions
copy.thesis: These standards inform the diagnostic; they do not define the bands.
copy.advisory_disclaimer: This is an advisory readiness diagnostic, not a certification. It describes structural readiness against the AI-Readiness Standard; it is not a guarantee that any particular AI deployment will succeed.
copy.routing_cta: Close the gaps this diagnostic surfaced: the prioritized remediation list above is the starting point for a readiness engagement.

verdicts_by_overall_level.1: Ad hoc — establish accountability and a written policy before building.
verdicts_by_overall_level.2: Emerging — formalize ownership and name your AI-specific risks.
verdicts_by_overall_level.3: Defined — you have the foundations; invest in controls and measurement next.
verdicts_by_overall_level.4: Governed — strong footing; tighten assurance and third-party posture.
verdicts_by_overall_level.5: Optimized — continuous assurance in place; sustain and audit it.
